title: Rear drive shaft disassembly and assembly

blocks.text: Rear drive shaft disassembly and assembly

items: Parts to be disassembled shall be marked to ensure that they are assembled in the same position when assembled.

blocks.text: 1. Boot band removal

items: Be careful not to damage the boots.

blocks.text: Locking clip Raise the folded part (B) of the band (A). Raise the lever (C) and remove the locking clip band.
2. Inboard joint removal
Wipe grease from inboard joint (A). Remove the snap ring (B) from the inboard joint. Align inboard joint and bearing cage (C) and mark (D). Wrap with a rag (E) and remove the inboard joint.
3. Bearing removal
Mark (D) the drive shaft (A), bearing race (B), and bearing cage (C) to match. Remove the circlip (E) from the drive shaft. Remove the bearing race, bearing cage and steel ball (F).

items: Steel Be careful not to damage the ball.

blocks.text: 4. Inboard boot removal
Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the spline to prevent damage to the inboard boot (A). Remove the inboard boot from the drive shaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
1. Boot band removal

items: Be careful not to damage the boots.

blocks.text: Locking clip Raise the folded part (B) of the band (A). Raise the lever (C) and remove the locking clip band.
2. Outboard joint removal
Move the outboard boot (A) to the inboard joint side.

items: Be careful not to damage the boots.

blocks.text: Wipe off grease in the drive shaft (A) and outboard joint (B). Place a scale (C) on the end of the outboard joint and align it with the driveshaft and mark (D).

Wrap the drive shaft (A) with a rag (B) and press it down with a vise (C) so as not to damage it. Attach the special tool to the outboard joint (D) and pull the outboard joint off the drive shaft. Remove the drive shaft from the vise.
Remove the stop ring (B) from the drive shaft (A).
3. Outboard boot removal
Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the splines to prevent damage to the outboard boot (A). Remove the outboard boot from the driveshaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
1. development

items: All disassembled parts other than rubber parts shall be cleaned with washing oil and washed with an air gun to remove the oil.

blocks.text: 1. Inboard boot mounting

Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the spline to prevent damage to the inboard boot (A). Thread the new locking clip band (D) onto the drive shaft. Install the inboard boot onto the driveshaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
2. Bearing Mounting

Align the drive shaft (A), bearing race (B), and bearing cage (C) with the alignment marks (D) and install the bearing race, bearing cage, and steel ball (E). Install the circlip (F) into the groove on the drive shaft.
3. Inboard joint installation
Lubricate inboard joints and inboard boots with grease.

Align the inboard joint (A) with the alignment mark (C) on the bearing cage (B) and install the inboard joint. Install the new snap ring (D) onto the inboard joint. Place the inboard boot (E) over the inboard joint.

items: After the inboard joint is assembled, be careful not to point the inboard joint downward as it will fall.
Care should be taken to prevent grease from adhering to the boot mounting groove.

blocks.text: Align the inboard boot (A) with the groove (D) in the inboard joint (B) and drive shaft (C) and cover it.

items: Care should be taken to prevent grease from adhering to the boot mounting groove.

blocks.text: 4. Between inboard and outboard joints Length adjustment
Adjust the space between the inboard and outboard joints to the specified dimensions.

items: The air pressure in the boot shall be atmospheric when the length between joints (A) is within the standard value to prevent deformation of the boot.

blocks.text: 5. Boot Band Installation
Wrap the drive shaft side locking clip band (A) around the inboard boot (B) and bend the lever (C). Fold the clip section (D).
Assemble the inboard joint side locking clip band (E) in the same manner. Make sure the band is not loose and wipe off any grease or other contaminants on the boot or drive shaft surface.
1. Outboard Boot Mounting

Wrap the vinyl tape (B) from the end of the drive shaft (A) to the spline. Thread the new locking clip band (C) onto the drive shaft. Install the outboard boot (D) onto the driveshaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
2. Outboard joint installation
Check the size of the new stop ring.

items: Be sure to check the size of the stop ring, as installing a stop ring other than the specified one will damage the driveshaft and the vehicle.

blocks.text: Install a new stop ring (B) in the groove (A) on the drive shaft.
Fill the outboard joints with 20 g of the specified grease.
Insert the drive shaft (C) until the stop ring (A) touches the outboard joint (B).
Holding the outboard joint and driveshaft vertically, drop them by their own weight from a height of about 10 cm above the floor and install the stop ring in the ring groove in the outboard joint.

items: Spread a rag on the floor and be careful not to damage the drive shaft end (threaded part).

blocks.text: Place a scale (B) on the end of the outboard joint (A) and check that it is aligned with the alignment mark (C) on the drive shaft.

items: Ensure that the stop ring is securely in the mounting groove of the outboard joint to prevent damage to the driveshaft or vehicle.

blocks.text: Lubricate grease in outboard joints and outboard boots.
Align the outboard boot (A) with the groove (D) in the outboard joint (B) and drive shaft (C) and cover it.

items: Care should be taken to prevent grease from adhering to the boot mounting groove.

blocks.text: 3. Between inboard and outboard joints Length adjustment
Adjust the space between the inboard and outboard joints to the specified dimensions.

items: The air pressure in the boot shall be atmospheric when the length between joints (A) is within the standard value to prevent deformation of the boot.

blocks.text: 4. Boot Band Installation
Wrap the drive shaft side locking clip band (A) around the outboard boot (B) and bend the lever (C). Fold the clip section (D).
Assemble the locking clip band (E) on the outboard joint side in the same manner. Make sure the band is not loose and wipe off any grease or other contaminants on the boot or drive shaft surface.
